actions-list.actionItem: Pursuant to the order of yesterday, the Journal of the proceedings of the Senate was deemed approved to date.
Pursuant to the order of yesterday, the morning hour being deemed expired, and the times for the recognition of the two leaders being reserved.
The following transactions of morning business occurred on today.

S. 2 (Sen. Graham):
        An original bill to provide for reconciliation pursuant to title II of S. Con. Res. 33.

        --Considered by Senate.

        --Motion by Senator Schumer to commit to Senate Committee on the Judiciary with instructions rejected in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 49 - 50. Record Vote Number:                                 137      


        --Motion by Senator Ossoff to commit to Senate Committee on the Judiciary with instructions rejected in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 47 - 50. Record Vote Number:                                 141      


        --Motion by Senator Warnock to commit to Senate Committee on the Judiciary with instructions rejected in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 46 - 52. Record Vote Number:                                 147      


        --Motion by Senator Wyden to commit to Senate Committee on the Judiciary with instructions rejected in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 48 - 51. Record Vote Number:                                 153      


        --Motion by Senator Rosen to commit to Senate Committee on the Judiciary with instructions rejected in Senate by Voice Vote.

        --Motion by Senator Wyden to commit to Senate Committee on the Judiciary with instructions rejected in Senate by Voice Vote.

        --Passed Senate with an amendment by Yea-Nay Vote. 52 - 47. Record Vote Number:                                 163      

         

S.Amdt. 5451 (Sen. Sanders):
        To place reasonable limits on contributions to Super PACs which make independent expenditures.

        --Amendment SA 5451 proposed by Senator Sanders to Amendment SA 5453.

        --Point of order that the amendment violates section 313(b)(1)(c) of the CBA raised in Senate with respect to amendment SA 5451.

        --Motion to waive all budgetary discipline with respect to amendment SA 5451, not having achieved 60 votes in the affirmative, rejected in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 45 - 53. Record Vote Number:                                                     148          


        --Amendment SA 5451 ruled out of order by the chair.
        

S.Amdt. 5452 (Sen. Tillis):
        To reallocate funds that would have been used for the Anti-Weaponization Fund to fraud enforcement.

        --Amendment SA 5452 proposed by Senator Tillis.

        --Point of order that the amendment violates section 305(f) of the CBA raised in Senate with respect to amendment SA 5452.

        --Motion to waive all applicable budgetary discipline with respect to amendment SA 5452, not having achieved 60 votes in the affirmative, rejected in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 15 - 84. Record Vote Number:                                                     138          


        --Amendment SA 5452 ruled out of order by the chair.
        

S.Amdt. 5453 (Sen. Graham):
        In the nature of a substitute.

        --Considered by Senate.

        --Amendment SA 5453 agreed to in Senate by Voice Vote.
        

S.Amdt. 5457 (Sen. Coons):
        To prohibit the Department of Justice from using taxpayer funds to make settlement payments to individuals convicted of assaulting law enforcement officers on January 6, 2021.

        --Amendment SA 5457 proposed by Senator Coons to Amendment SA 5453.

        --Point of order that the amendment violates section 313(b)(1)(c) of the CBA raised in Senate with respect to amendment SA 5457.

        --Motion to waive all budgetary discipline with respect to amendment SA 5457, not having achieved 60 votes in the affirmative, rejected in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 54 - 45. Record Vote Number:                                                     158          


        --Amendment SA 5457 ruled out of order by the chair.
        

S.Amdt. 5463 (Sen. Cortez Masto):
        To appropriate amounts for local law enforcement hiring programs.

        --Amendment SA 5463 proposed by Senator Cortez Masto to Amendment SA 5453.

        --Amendment SA 5463 not agreed to in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 45 - 53. Record Vote Number:                                                     160          

        

S.Amdt. 5485 (Sen. Baldwin):
        To redirect funding for U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement to the Child Care and Development Block Grant program.

        --Amendment SA 5485 proposed by Senator Baldwin to Amendment SA 5453.

        --Point of order that the amendment violates section 313(b)(1)(c) of the CBA raised in Senate with respect to amendment SA 5485.

        --Motion to waive all budgetary discipline with respect to amendment SA 5803, not having achieved 60 votes in the affirmative, rejected in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 46 - 53. Record Vote Number:                                                     149          


        --Amendment SA 5485 ruled out of order by the chair.
        

S.Amdt. 5501 (Sen. Hickenlooper):
        To prohibit the distribution of Federal funds to individuals convicted of certain crimes.

        --Amendment SA 5501 proposed by Senator Hickenlooper.

        --Point of order that the amendment violates section 313(b)(1)(c) of the CBA raised in Senate with respect to amendment SA 5501.

        --Motion to waive all budgetary discipline with respect to amendment SA 5501, not having achieved 60 votes in the affirmative, rejected in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 51 - 47. Record Vote Number:                                                     145          


        --Amendment SA 5591 ruled out of order by the chair.
        

S.Amdt. 5506 (Sen. Hirono):
        To transfer amounts for youth mentoring programs of the Department of Justice.

        --Amendment SA 5506 proposed by Senator Hirono to Amendment SA 5453.

        --Amendment SA 5506 not agreed to in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 46 - 53. Record Vote Number:                                                     154          

        

S.Amdt. 5512 (Sen. Merkley):
        To provide a clarification relating to, and to prohibit the use of funds for, the White House Ballroom.

        --Amendment SA 5512 proposed by Senator Merkley.

        --Point of order that the amendment violates section 305(b)(1)(c) of the CBA raised in Senate with respect to amendment SA 5512.

        --Motion to waive all budgetary discipline with respect to amendment SA 5512, not having achieved 60 votes in the affirmative, rejected in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 53 - 46. Record Vote Number:                                                     139          


        --Amendment SA 5512 ruled out of order by the chair.
        

S.Amdt. 5514 (Sen. Reed):
        To provide funding for the HOME Investment Partnerships program.

        --Amendment SA 5514 proposed by Senator Reed to Amendment SA 5453.

        --Point of order that the amendment violates section 313(b)(1)(c) of the CBA raised in Senate with respect to amendment SA 5514.

        --Motion to waive all budgetary discipline with respect to amendment SA 5514, not having achieved 60 votes in the affirmative, rejected in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 46 - 53. Record Vote Number:                                                     140          


        --Amendment SA 5514 ruled out of order by the chair.
        

S.Amdt. 5535 (Sen. Hassan):
        To help build 7,000,000 new homes.

        --Amendment SA 5535 proposed by Senator Hassan to Amendment SA 5453.

        --Point of order that the amendment violates section 313(b)(1)(c) of the CBA raised in Senate with respect to amendment SA 5535.

        --Motion to waive all budgetary discipline with respect to amendment SA 5535, not having achieved 60 votes in the affirmative, rejected in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 46 - 52. Record Vote Number:                                                     142          


        --Amendment SA 5535 ruled out of order by the chair.
        

S.Amdt. 5545 (Sen. Kim):
        To make appropriations for the conduct of inspections of the Delaney Hall Detention Facility.

        --Amendment SA 5545 proposed by Senator Kim to Amendment SA 5453.

        --Point of order that the amendment violates section 302(f) of the CBA raised in Senate with respect to amendment SA 5545.

        --Motion to waive all budgetary discipline with respect to amendment SA 5545, not having achieved 60 votes in the affirmative, rejected in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 46 - 53. Record Vote Number:                                                     146          


        --Amendment SA 5545 ruled out of order by the chair.
        

S.Amdt. 5556 (Sen. Warner):
        To prohibit an individual from acting as Director of National Intelligence if such individual is simultaneously serving as the head of any other Federal department or agency.

        --Amendment SA 5556 proposed by Senator Warner to Amendment SA 5453.

        --Point of order that the amendment violates section 313(b)(1)(c) of the CBA raised in Senate with respect to amendment SA 5556.

        --Motion to waive all budgetary discipline with respect to amendment SA 5556, not having achieved 60 votes in the affirmative, rejected in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 49 - 49. Record Vote Number:                                                     143          


        --Amendment SA 5556 ruled out of order by the chair.
        

S.Amdt. 5632 (Sen. Van Hollen):
        To prohibit the use of funds to provide payments to certain individuals.

        --Amendment SA 5632 proposed by Senator Van Hollen to Amendment SA 5453.

        --Point of order that the amendment violates section 313(b)(1)(a) of the CBA raised in Senate with respect to amendment SA 5632.

        --Motion to waive all budgetary discipline with respect to amendment SA 5632, not having achieved 60 votes in the affirmative, rejected in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 53 - 46. Record Vote Number:                                                     161          


        --Amendment SA 5632 ruled out of order by the chair.
        

S.Amdt. 5740 (Sen. Schiff):
        To provide for limitations on judgments, awards, and compromise settlements under section 1304 of title 31, United States Code.

        --Amendment SA 5740 proposed by Senator Schiff to Amendment SA 5453.

        --Point of order that the amendment violates section 313(b)(1)(a) of the CBA raised in Senate with respect to amendment SA 5740.

        --Motion to waive all budgetary discipline with respect to amendment SA 5740, not having achieved 60 votes in the affirmative, rejected in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 51 - 48. Record Vote Number:                                                     162          


        --Amendment SA 5740 ruled out of order by the chair.
        

S.Amdt. 5763 (Sen. Peters):
        To rescind unobligated balances under the One Big Beautiful Bill Act.

        --Amendment SA 5763 proposed by Senator Peters to Amendment SA 5453.

        --Amendment SA 5763 not agreed to in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 46 - 53. Record Vote Number:                                                     155          

        

S.Amdt. 5779 (Sen. Graham):
        To ensure only citizens are registered to vote in elections for Federal office, and to require voters to provide photo identification, and for other purposes.

        --Amendment SA 5779 proposed by Senator Graham to Amendment SA 5453.

        --Point of order that the amendment violates section 313(b)(1)(c) of the CBA raised in Senate with respect to amendment SA 5779.

        --Motion to waive all budgetary discipline with respect to amendment SA 5779, not having achieved 60 votes in the affirmative, rejected in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 48 - 50. Record Vote Number:                                                     144          


        --Amendment SA 5779 ruled out of order by the chair.
        

S.Amdt. 5803 (Sen. Booker):
        To support the work performed by the Immigration Detention Ombudsman.

        --Amendment SA 5803 proposed by Senator Booker to Amendment SA 5453.

        --Point of order that the amendment violates section 302(f) of the CBA raised in Senate with respect to amendment SA 5803.

        --Motion to waive all budgetary discipline with respect to amendment SA 5803, not having achieved 60 votes in the affirmative, rejected in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 46 - 53. Record Vote Number:                                                     150          


        --Amendment SA 5803 ruled out of order by the chair.
        

S.Amdt. 5804 (Sen. Lee):
        To ensure only citizens are registered to vote in elections for Federal office, and to require voters to provide photo identification.

        --Amendment SA 5804 proposed by Senator Lee to Amendment SA 5453.

        --Point of order that the amendment violates section 305(b)(2) of the CBA raised in Senate with respect to amendment SA 5804.

        --Motion to waive all budgetary discipline with respect to amendment SA 5804, not having achieved 60 votes in the affirmative, rejected in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 50 - 49. Record Vote Number:                                                     151          


        --Amendment SA 5804 ruled out of order by the chair.
        

S.Amdt. 5806 (Sen. Durbin):
        To repurpose funds previously appropriated for U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement for the timely processing of DACA applications and related employment authorization documents and to prohibit the use of funds to arrest, detain, deport, or remove DACA recipients.

        --Amendment SA 5806 proposed by Senator Durbin to Amendment SA 5453.

        --Point of order that the amendment violates section 313(1)(a) of the CBA raised in Senate with respect to amendment SA 5806.

        --Motion to waive all budgetary discipline with respect to amendment SA 5806, not having achieved 60 votes in the affirmative, rejected in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 48 - 51. Record Vote Number:                                                     152          


        --Amendment SA 5806 ruled out of order by the chair.

        --Section 204(b) of Amendment SA 5806 was stricken.

        --Proposed amendment SA 5806 withdrawn in Senate.
        

S.Amdt. 5808 (Sen. Padilla):
        To make appropriations for transparency and accountability.

        --Amendment SA 5808 proposed by Senator Padilla to Amendment SA 5453.

        --Amendment SA 5808 not agreed to in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 46 - 53. Record Vote Number:                                                     157          

        

S.Amdt. 5812 (Sen. Cassidy):
        To provide compensation to law enforcement officers who defended the United States Capitol during the events that occurred at or near the United States Capitol on January 6, 2021, and for other purposes.

        --Amendment SA 5812 proposed by Senator Cassidy to Amendment SA 5453.

        --Point of order that the amendment violates section 313(b)(1)(d) of the CBA raised in Senate with respect to amendment SA 5812.

        --Motion to waive all budgetary discipline with respect to amendment SA 5812, not having achieved 60 votes in the affirmative, rejected in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 52 - 47. Record Vote Number:                                                     159          


        --Amendment SA 5812 ruled out of order by the chair.
        

S.Amdt. 5813 (Sen. Gallego):
        To make funds available for the timely adjudication of DACA renewal applications.

        --Amendment SA 5813 proposed by Senator Gallego to Amendment SA 5453.

        --Amendment SA 5813 not agreed to in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 47 - 52. Record Vote Number:                                                     156          

      

    

S. 1318 (Sen. Moran):
        A bill to direct the American Battle Monuments Commission to establish a program to identify American-Jewish servicemembers buried in United States military cemeteries overseas under markers that incorrectly represent their religion and heritage, and for other purposes.

        --Motion to proceed to consideration of the House message to accompany S. 1318 rejected in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 47 - 52. Record Vote Number:                                 164

PN851-4:
        Anthony W. Mattivi, of Kansas, to be United States District Judge for the District of Kansas

        --Motion to proceed to executive session to consideration of nomination agreed to in Senate by Voice Vote.

        --Cloture motion presented in Senate.
By unanimous consent, on the request of Mr. Thune, at 5:15 a.m., the Senate adjourned, under its order of today, until 3 p.m. on Monday, June 8, 2026.
